# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# ======================== Elasticsearch Configuration =========================
#
# For a full list of configuration options, see the documentation:
# https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/settings.html

# ---------------------------------- Cluster -----------------------------------
#
cluster.name: production-elasticsearch-cluster

# ------------------------------------ Node ------------------------------------
#
# Use a descriptive name for the node.
#
node.name: ${HOSTNAME}

# ----------------------------------- Paths ------------------------------------
#
# Path to store the data (multiple paths can be configured)
#
path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch
#
# Path to store the log files
#
path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch

# ---------------------------------- Network -----------------------------------
#
# Bind to all network interfaces, making it accessible externally.
# For production, consider using specific internal IPs if possible,
# and restrict access via firewall rules.
#
network.host: 0.0.0.0

# Set the HTTP port for client communication.
#
http.port: 9200

# Set the transport port for inter-node communication.
#
transport.port: 9300

# --------------------------------- Discovery ----------------------------------
#
# Pass an initial list of master-eligible nodes for cluster formation.
# Replace with actual hostnames or IP addresses of your master-eligible nodes.
#
discovery.seed_hosts: ["es-master-01", "es-master-02", "es-master-03"]

#
# When starting a new cluster, provide a list of master-eligible nodes
# that will be used to bootstrap the cluster.
#
cluster.initial_master_nodes: ["es-master-01", "es-master-02", "es-master-03"]

# ---------------------------------- Security ----------------------------------
#
# For production clusters, enabling X-Pack security is highly recommended.
# This typically involves setting up TLS for HTTP and transport, and role-based access control.
# Uncomment and configure these settings based on your security requirements.
#
# xpack.security.enabled: true
# xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
# xpack.security.http.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
# xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/http.p12
# xpack.security.http.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/http.p12
# xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
# xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
# xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/transport.p12
# xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/transport.p12

# ------------------------------------ CORS ------------------------------------
#
# Enable cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) for your web applications.
#
http.cors.enabled: true
#
# Specify the allowed origins. For production, replace "*" with your actual
# web application domains (e.g., "https://mywebapp.com, https://anotherapp.com")
# Using "*" allows requests from any origin, which might be too permissive.
#
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"
#
# Allow specific HTTP methods for CORS requests.
#
http.cors.allow-methods: OPTIONS, HEAD, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
#
# Allow specific headers for CORS requests.
#
http.cors.allow-headers: X-Requested-With, X-Auth-Token, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization, Access-Control-Allow-Headers, Accept
#
# Allow credentials (like cookies or HTTP authentication) if needed by web applications.
#
http.cors.allow-credentials: true
#
# Set the maximum age (in seconds) that the results of a preflight request can be cached.
#
http.cors.max-age: 86400

# ---------------------------------- Memory ----------------------------------
#
# Lock JVM memory to prevent swapping. This is crucial for production stability.
# Ensure that 'bootstrap.memory_lock: true' is also present in jvm.options or
# set as an environment variable (e.g., systemd service file).
#
bootstrap.memory_lock: true
